---
name: ddx
description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "what's blocking the queue", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
---

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
  `ddx bead status`.
- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  raw passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Worker** — a `ddx work` / `ddx try` process scoped to a specific project
  root. Do not call every `ddx work` process a "DDx worker" when multiple
  projects are visible. If the user says "DDx worker" without a qualifier,
  assume they mean the worker for the current/requested project. Say "worker
  for `<project>`" or "DDx-powered worker"; reserve "DDx repo worker" for work
  whose project root is the DDx repository.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| "what should I work on next", "what's blocking the queue", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", broad queue orientation or planning | `reference/interactive.md` |
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "start the worker", "start N workers and watch", "monitor the drain", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "assess bead readiness", "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. `ddx try` does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

## Links out

- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
  executions, and skills features.
- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:
  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).

data.text: I’ve got the DDx skill router loaded. Next I’m reading the execution-specific guidance and then I’ll inspect the bead record plus the code/test paths that mention “fingerprint” so I can bind the bead to concrete evidence instead of guessing.

data.output:
# Work — Drain the Queue, Execute, Verify, Close

## Mode note

This reference covers **`bead_execution` mode**: explicit worker commands such as
`ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", or "start the worker".

If the user said "what should I work on next?", "what's blocking the queue?", or
"work the queue" without an explicit bead ID or `ddx work` command, they are in
**`interactive-steward`** mode — see `reference/interactive.md`. The steward
plans; the worker executes. Do not instruct manual ready-bead implementation in
response to broad orientation prompts.

---

"Doing work" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,
run one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on
success or leave it available for a future eligible retry.

## Default surface: `ddx work`

```bash
ddx work
```

`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.
`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single agent invocation primitive. DDx
owns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; Fizeau owns concrete
routing, provider/model discovery, alias resolution, fuzzy matching, catalog
lookups, and transcript/session rendering.

Flags worth knowing:

- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).
- `--watch` — keep running after the current ready queue drains; wait for newly-ready beads.
- `--idle-interval <dur>` — sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode (default 30s).
- `--min-power <n>` / `--max-power <n>` — requested agent power bounds.
- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the service's reported
  power bands.
- `--harness <name>` / `--provider <name>` / `--model <ref>` / `--profile <name>` — passthrough constraints only. DDx sends them
  unchanged and does not route on them.

## Pre-claim Intake and Silent-Idle Diagnosis

The worker's pre-claim intake hook evaluates a `readiness_checks` payload before
claiming a bead. The canonical verdict forms are:

- JSON bool `true` -> `pass`
- JSON bool `false` -> `fail`
- JSON strings are passed through after trimming
- `null` or absent -> empty

`ClassifyReadinessWithMode`
(`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go:56-115`) is the mapping layer
between readiness classifications and worker behavior:

- `system_unready` / `intake_error` are hard errors from the hook, but the
  worker fail-opens and skips the claim instead of parking the bead.
- `needs_refine` is warn-only in warn mode and becomes operator-attention /
  park in block mode.
- `operator_required` parks the bead.
- `needs_split` parks the bead for decomposition.

When the worker appears to idle on a full queue, inspect `loop.idle` events
first. That event stream is the diagnostic surface for a silent stall. Repeated
identical blocker details are escalated after
`preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold` cycles (`ddx-df77e668`), which should convert
the stall into a `loop.operator_attention` signal instead of letting the worker
spin forever. The main blocker codes to distinguish are
`preclaim_systemic` and `preclaim_tracker_contention`.

If the queue is still making claims but the rate is suspiciously low, use the
claim-success-rate warning knobs `--claim-rate-window` and
`--claim-rate-threshold` to surface that condition.

## Are workers running for this project?

When someone asks "is the worker still working beads?" or "how is the queue
progressing?", do not answer with a global `ps aux | grep -E 'ddx work|ddx try'`
scan. That returns workers from every checkout on the host and falsely
signals progress on whichever project happens to be asked about.

```bash
ddx work status                # live workers scoped to this project
ddx work status --json         # machine-readable
ddx work status --all-projects # explicit cross-project escape hatch
```

`ddx work status` defaults to the current project root (CLI flag → env
`DDX_PROJECT_ROOT` → CWD git root). It reports pid, age, command line,
project root, and — when inferable from argv or an isolated execute-bead
worktree — the active bead ID. Always name the project root the answer
applies to; never substitute a worker from a different repository as
evidence that this project is progressing.

When multiple repositories have workers, report them project-first:

```text
<project-root>
  pid=<pid> bead=<bead-id> phase=<phase>
```

Use "worker for `<project-root>`" for project-scoped status. Use
"DDx-powered worker" for a generic `ddx work` process. Use "DDx repo worker"
only when the project root is the DDx repository. If the user says "DDx
worker" without a qualifier, assume the current/requested project and do not
surface workers from other projects.

`--all-projects` is the only sanctioned way to ask "what ddx workers
exist anywhere on this host?" — use it only when the question is
explicitly cross-project.

## Primitive: `ddx try`

For targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:

```bash
ddx try <bead-id>
ddx try <bead-id> --from <rev>      # base commit override
ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land
```

`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`
for the actual agent invocation.

## Pick by default, not by ID

Under normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks
the top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only
pin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try <id>`) or
when the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to
override.

## Starting and monitoring workers in the background

When the user asks you to **start workers and watch progress**, do
not invent your own `tail | grep` pipeline — the obvious shapes have
all bitten in the field. Use these vetted patterns.

### One worker, foreground drain

If the user just wants to drain once and read the result at the end,
run `ddx work` directly and let it print. Don't wrap with `head`,
`tail`, or `tee` — pipes to those tools buffer stdout for the entire
run, so the user sees nothing during execution and everything at the
end.

### One or more workers, watch live

```bash
# Start each worker, one log per worker:
ddx work > /tmp/ddx-worker1.log 2>&1 &   # repeat for worker2, etc.

# Stream with per-worker prefixes (drops the `==>` separator noise
# that `tail -F` injects when switching files):
(tail -F /tmp/ddx-worker1.log 2>/dev/null | awk '{print "w1: "$0; fflush()}' &
 tail -F /tmp/ddx-worker2.log 2>/dev/null | awk '{print "w2: "$0; fflush()}' &
 wait) | grep -E --line-buffered \
  "^w[0-9]+: (▶|✗|✓|→ |worker exited|attempts:|failed:|landing worktree has|lock_contention|panic|FATAL|excluded|caller_excluded|ladder exhausted|escalation|powerClass=|do route|i/o timeout|connection refused|provider error)"
```

Why those specific tokens — together they cover **every actionable
event** in a drain:

- `▶ <bead>` — a new bead was claimed. `✗` and `✓` close it.
- `→` — outcome arrow, also follows a bead-state transition.
- `worker exited` / `attempts:` / `failed:` — end-of-drain summary.
- `landing worktree has` — pre-claim hook hit a stale index; queue
  is jammed if recovery doesn't kick in.
- `lock_contention`, `panic`, `FATAL` — infrastructure problems.
- `excluded`, `caller_excluded`, `ladder exhausted`,
  `escalation`, `powerClass=` — the escalation path actually
  firing; absence on a failed bead means the loop is silently
  no-op'ing instead of climbing the ladder.
- `do route` — the implementation-phase routing decision, surfaces
  the provider/model that's about to be hit. Without this in the
  alternation, the monitor goes quiet during the most interesting
  window (provider call) and only resurfaces on success/failure.
- `i/o timeout`, `connection refused`, `provider error` — transport
  failures. Two in a row to the same `(provider, model)` is the
  signature of routing that isn't honoring exclusions.

### What NOT to do

- **Don't wrap `ddx work` in `| tail -N`** to "see the last N
  lines". The pipe buffers until the producer exits, so you see
  nothing for the whole run. Redirect to a file and tail the file
  separately.
- **Don't grep `^==>` into the alternation.** Without the awk
  prefix, `tail -F file1 file2` emits `==> filename <==` whenever
  the active file switches — every switch becomes a noise event in
  a Monitor pipeline. Strip those, or prefix lines with `awk`.
- **Don't tail without `--line-buffered`/`fflush()`.** Pipe
  buffering means events arrive in 4KB chunks (often minutes apart)
  instead of as they happen. The `awk` form above flushes per line.
- **Don't share one log file across workers.** Interleaved output
  destroys attributability and any chance of debugging which worker
  hit what.

## Verify independently before closing

**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the
agent's self-report. Before closing a bead:

1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's
   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.
2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from
   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the
   attempt.
3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?
   (`[ddx-<id>]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)

If all three pass: close the bead.

## Close on success, unclaim on failure

DDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome
maps to a concrete follow-up action:

During a live `ddx work` / `ddx try` attempt, the current bead's lifecycle is
orchestrator-owned. The worker executing that bead must not run
`ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`,
`ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>` for the
current bead. The Step 0 split-first path still allows child-bead
`ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and the parent-note update
`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.

| Outcome | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |
| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |

`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running
`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update <id>
--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another
worker can pick it up.

**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or
unclaims.

## Testing expectations for bead implementations

When an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests
that exercise the new code:

- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party
  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call
  sequences test implementation, not behavior.
- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost
  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real
  HTTP via a local test server).
- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock
  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their
  scope.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a server-managed subprocess launch path for StartExecuteLoop in cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-464. Record nonzero PID and PGID on WorkerRecord and status.json. Keep the existing in-process path only where tests explicitly inject it, and do not make externally reported workers look server-owned.

NON-SCOPE

# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.

10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.707210124Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-387a0178","depends_on_id":"ddx-0b8780db","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/coordinator.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/review.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/review_triage.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/disposition.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go         -> cli/internal/agent/work/drain.go
    cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/mirror.go

  Rename identifiers: ExecuteBeadWorker -> work.Worker, ExecuteBeadWorker.Run -> work.Worker.Drain, ExecuteBead -> try.Attempt (FEAT-006 + FEAT-010).

  Add cli/cmd/try.go with cobra command "ddx try <bead-id>" invoking try.Attempt directly (FEAT-010 surface).

  Update FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 spec docs to reflect new package paths. Keep ExecuteBead* shims as typedefs; deletion is C14.

NON-SCOPE
  - Behavior changes to the drain loop or attempt logic.
  - Deletion of ExecuteBead* typedefs (C14).
  - ddx-dc157075 file-path reference migration if that bead has not yet landed.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: file and identifier names should reflect the subsystem boundary and keep responsibilities separated.
- P5: ddx try as a first-class CLI surface makes the worker/attempt state easier for operators to see.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. 3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"9f2c30fc1ecf1e949214fc4782572a47d6ffc3d7","created_at":"2026-06-08T16:59:04.8421817Z","description":"PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-eea2dfca is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead only adds the Niflheim observation as regression coverage and decision/audit wiring.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|PreClaim|RetryEscalation|PreClaimDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. 2. Inter is actually LOADED: a Playwright assertion that `document.fonts` includes Inter (not merely declared) and `getComputedStyle(h1).fontFamily` resolves to the loaded family; re-baseline `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` snapshots.
3. The `semanticColors` alias layer is retained (class names unchanged); design:export remains the source of truth.
4. cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test && bun run test:e2e green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-06-05T17:51:32.412466203Z","description":"PROBLEM: Two issues mask the intended design system (`.stitch/DESIGN.md`: warm-paper brutalist). (1) `cli/internal/server/frontend/design-tokens.json` is the STALE generic Stitch export (`tertiary:#8e35a3` purple, `surface:#f9fafb`, Inter for all roles) — its warm palette only reaches the app because `cli/internal/server/frontend/tailwind.config.js:8-37` hardcodes `semanticColors` that override the JSON. So `bun run design:export` regenerates GENERIC values; a reader cannot tell which file is authoritative. (2) The typeface (`Inter`, tailwind.config.js:39-46) is SPECIFIED but never LOADED (no @font-face/@fontsource/Google Fonts anywhere) -> renders the OS system-sans fallback.

ROOT CAUSE: the design:export pipeline emits the generic default token DATA (not DESIGN.md's warm palette); and no font asset is imported. Per codex: `semanticColors` is the load-bearing alias layer (KEEP it) — only the generated token DATA is stale. Operator decision: load Inter (do NOT introduce a new display font — that would be a spec change).

PROPOSED FIX: Regenerate `design-tokens.json` from `.stitch/DESIGN.md` (warm palette: #3B5B7A lever-steel, #F4EFE6 cream, etc.) so the export pipeline is the source of truth and carries the correct values; KEEP the `semanticColors` alias layer in tailwind.config.js (do not delete it; ensure it reads the regenerated tokens rather than shadowing stale ones). Add Inter via `@fontsource-variable/inter` (`bun add`), import in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/app.css`, so all non-mono roles render Inter instead of system fallback.

NON-SCOPE: introducing a distinctive display font (operator chose Inter-only); off-palette utility migration (separate bead); deleting the semanticColors The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260505T164659-abc25e07","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":285426,"output_tokens":2755,"total_tokens":288181,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":34343,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=288181 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"The bead is too large for this execution depth and cannot be cleanly split further here. 3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"6484faf94fc9839d963baaf6ee7a1d20fb83f321","created_at":"2026-06-08T18:36:52.319292895Z","description":"PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-bced4e39 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this is typed classification and reconciliation behavior, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineer
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|LandCoordination|RetryEscalation|LandCoordinationDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:103:{"acceptance":"1. `TestReadinessChecksSchema` compiles the shared readiness-checks schema and validates fixtures for bool, string, null, and absent verdicts.
2. `TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema` or an equivalent prompt/schema contract test proves the producer prompt names the same `readiness_checks` shape validated by the schema.
3. `make readiness-schema` or the repo's equivalent CI target runs the schema test and fails if the schema, prompt contract, or decoder accepted forms drift.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestReadinessChecksSchema|TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema|TestPreClaimReadinessPromptSpecifiesOutputShapes'` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"cebefeb75700987553e094eff5db0fe1dc790a87","created_at":"2026-05-27T05:50:46.132570402Z","description":"PROBLEM
The readiness producer and pre-claim intake consumer can drift on the `readiness_checks` JSON shape. That drift caused the parent incident when one side emitted `verdict` as a bool and the other side expected a string.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The pre-claim prompt specifies the expected output shape in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:616-621`, while the Go consumer decodes `ReadinessChecks` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:68` and `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:781-814`. Without a shared schema and CI gate, prompt text and decoder behavior can evolve independently.

PROPOSED FIX
Define `readiness_checks` in a shared JSON schema file in the agent package, make the prompt/schema/decoder tests consume the same schema, and add a CI-accessible Make target that fails on schema drift. The schema must allow exactly the verdict forms accepted by the decoder from the decoder child: bool, string, null, or absent.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement the decoder tolerance itself except as needed to align with the existing decoder contract; this child depends on the decoder child. Do not implement same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warnings, README documentation, Fizeau route/harness configuration, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not introduce a network-dependent schema validation step. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES
Depends on the decoder child that canonicalizes bool/string/null/absent verdicts.","events_attachment":"ddx-066cceaf/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-066cceaf","issue_type":"task","labels":["ac-quality:needs-refinement","area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","severity:high"],"parent":"ddx-f9ddaa68","priority":0,"queue-rank":990,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-916746a9","status":"closed","title":"Lock readiness_checks schema behind shared CI validation","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:31.379015443Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:136:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.

2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.

3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.

4. **First-pass perf targets.**
   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.

5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**
   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.

6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.

7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.

8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.

9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"d4874cfd75778a35969c330285e33a126e359108","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:47:04.443214848Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0a33bc5f","depends_on_id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:21:53Z"}],"description":"## Observed

The Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be "an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model" — which is not what the current implementation does.

## Ground truth (current implementation)

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —

- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.
- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.
- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.
- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.

## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)

- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: "how is harness/provider/model X doing?" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.
- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).
- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.

## Proposed direction

### Part 1 — Reshape the data source

- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.
- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.
- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.
- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.

### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)

- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.
- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.
- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.

### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)

- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.

### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path

- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.
- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.

## Out of scope

- The `comparisons` / "compare arms" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).
- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).

ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder rejects the readiness payload when a readiness_checks entry's `verdict` is a JSON bool, emitting `decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string`. Under warn-only policy this skips every bead, idling a full fizeau queue (23 ready beads, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC). The identical error fingerprint (15155a1c...) across distinct bead_ids confirms a producer/consumer schema mismatch, not a bead defect.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go` — `preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict` (struct at :75) bound `verdict` to a plain string, so `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResultWithMode` (:688) and `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult` (:681) fail when the readiness producer emits `verdict:true`. A verdict may legitimately be a JSON bool, a JSON string (e.g. "fail"/"pass"/"ready"), or be omitted.

PROPOSED FIX
Give the field a dedicated `readinessVerdict` type (:91) with a custom UnmarshalJSON that accepts a JSON bool (coerced to a documented canonical lowercased string), a JSON string (trimmed), and JSON null/absent (empty), and rejects any other JSON kind with a descriptive error. Downstream comparisons in `failedReadinessReasons` (:823) and `readinessCheckEvidence` (:848) operate on the canonical lowercased string.

NON-SCOPE
Does NOT add the shared JSON schema or CI drift gate (covered by the schema-lock child). Does NOT change the worker same-fingerprint escalation (escalation child), the rolling claim-success-rate signal (claim-rate child), or README docs (docs child). ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder once failed to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when readiness_checks[].verdict arrived as a JSON bool, because the producer (readiness prompt) emitted bool while the consumer (Go decoder) parsed string. Under warn-only policy every claim attempt produced an identical-fingerprint intake_error warn and the worker idled the whole fizeau queue (23 ready beads, one daemon) indefinitely. The detail string was: "decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string". This child owns the schema-gate slice of parent ddx-61f6c57d: make the readiness payload shape a single enforced contract so this class of producer/consumer drift fails the build instead of silently wedging the queue at runtime.

ROOT CAUSE
The verdict shape contract lives in two places that can drift independently: the producer prompt string assembled in buildPreClaimIntakePrompt (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639-642) and the Go consumer readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:97-125), which coerces bool true->"pass", bool false->"fail", string->lowercased, null/absent->"". The canonical shape is declared in cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json (readiness_checks[].verdict is oneOf boolean/string/null). Without a CI gate binding all three together, a change to any one side reintroduces the wedge.

PROPOSED FIX
Keep cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json as the single source of truth for the readiness payload. Ensure (a) the schema accepts exactly the verdict forms readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON accepts, (b) the producer prompt continues to cite readinessChecksSchemaPath (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21,639-642) as authoritative, and (c) a CI gate fails on drift: the `readiness-schema` Makefile target (cli/Makefile:152-156) runs the schema tests and is invoked from .github/workflows/ci.yml:66-67 via `make readiness-schema`. The gate must fail if the schema, the prompt, or the decoder diverge on the verdict contract.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement the same-fingerprint warn escalation / silent-idle guard — that is the sibling child (worker loop escalation). Do not change the warn-only policy_mode semantics. Do not implement any other parent acceptance criterion. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint.","events_attachment":"ddx-1d071ef5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-1d071ef5","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","decomposed-child","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","severity:high"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-07-02T22:52:48Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"closed","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"closed","parent":"ddx-fd02cf6e","priority":0,"queue-rank":1140,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-500716b9","status":"closed","title":"Enforce shared readiness_checks schema gate in CI so producer prompt and Go decoder cannot drift on verdict type","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:32.042521375Z","work-failed-routes":[{"actual_power":5,"at":"2026-05-28T01:46:59Z","count":3,"endpoint":"http://bragi:1234/v1/chat/completions","model":"qwen3.5-27b","provider":"bragi","reason":"provider_connectivity","timeout_class":"bad gateway"}]}

`ddx work --watch` can reject every pre-claim readiness result when `readiness_checks[].verdict` is emitted as a JSON bool instead of the consumer's expected string shape. In the observed Fizeau queue, each ready bead produced the same `pre_claim_intake.warn` decode error and the worker idled indefinitely despite ready work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The readiness classification decoder consumes `readiness_checks` through `preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:75-80`, then uses `failedReadinessReasons` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:823-834` to interpret failed checks. The contract must explicitly tolerate bool, string, null, and absent verdict values at this boundary; otherwise producer/consumer schema drift turns a readiness payload into `intake_error` before a bead can execute.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement or verify a custom `readinessVerdict` decoder that accepts `true`, `false`, strings, `null`, and absent verdicts, coercing to a documented canonical form (`true` -> `pass`, `false` -> `fail`, string -> trimmed string, null/absent -> empty). Ensure the classification decode path exercises that type and that failed-check detection still treats canonical `fail` as the failure signal.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Fizeau route or harness configuration. Do not implement same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warnings, CI schema locking, README documentation, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not rely on `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/...` as load-bearing test input; inline fixtures in tests. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

TestReadinessChecksSchema in cli/internal/agent/readiness_schema_test.go validates every verdict form the decoder accepts (bool true, bool false, string, null, absent) against cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json AND asserts an object verdict is rejected, locking producer/consumer alignment.
2. The producer prompt buildPreClaimIntakePrompt (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:636-655) references the canonical schema path via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21), not a duplicated string literal.

4. lefthook run pre-commit passes","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"rev-ddx-2e3ad63d","created_at":"2026-05-28T09:08:21.2215798Z","description":"PROBLEM
The pre_claim_intake readiness payload schema can drift between the producer prompt and the Go decoder. That drift is exactly the class of bug that wedged the fizeau queue: the producer emitted readiness_checks[].verdict as a JSON bool while the consumer parsed a string, so every claim attempt failed with a warn-only rejection and the queue idled. There must be a single shared schema that both sides consume, plus a CI-run test that fails the moment the schema and decoder diverge.

ROOT CAUSE
- The producer prompt references the schema only by a path string in prose: cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639 (buildPreClaimIntakePrompt) writes "Canonical schema: "+readinessChecksSchemaPath, and the constant lives at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21. Because the prompt text and the schema file are independent, instructions can drift from the schema with nothing catching it.
- The consumer decoder accepts bool/string/null/absent verdict forms: readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:97-125 and preClaimReadinessChecksPayload.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:386-416.
- The schema verdict union is cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json:44-50 (oneOf boolean/string/null). Nothing fails CI if someone tightens the schema, or changes the decoder, so that the two diverge.

PROPOSED FIX
Keep cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json as the single source of truth consumed by both the producer prompt (via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant) and the consumer decoder. Ensure a CI-run test validates every verdict form the decoder accepts against the schema and asserts that an object verdict is rejected, so any tightening of the schema or change to the decoder that breaks alignment fails the suite. Confirm the producer prompt references the canonical path constant rather than a duplicated literal.

NON-SCOPE
- Do NOT implement the runtime same-fingerprint warn escalation guard (owned by the sibling child bead).
- Do NOT change the verdict coercion semantics (bool true->pass, bool false->fail, string->lowercased, null/absent->empty).
- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.

DEPENDENCIES: no deps.","events_attachment":"ddx-2e3ad63d/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2e3ad63d","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","decomposed","kind:reliability","needs-human-decomposition","observability","priority:reliability"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-07-02T22:52:48Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"closed","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"closed","parent":"ddx-fd02cf6e","priority":0,"queue-rank":1160,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"Lock readiness_checks schema as the shared producer/consumer source of truth with a CI drift gate","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:32.129982275Z"}

A live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle "Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.

This bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.

BACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE
The evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.

1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title "Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing".
- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.
- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.
- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.
- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:
  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`
  `powerClass=standard`
  `rationale: status: open`
  `orchestrator_action: decompose`
  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`
  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`
  `outcome_reason=decomposed`
- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.

2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title "Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8".
- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.
- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.
- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:
  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.
- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.

3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title "Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata".
- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:
  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`
- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.
- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.

4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.
- Recent Niflheim "success" attempts had empty review results:
  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.
  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.
  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision="success"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.
- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.
- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.
- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.
- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.
- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.
- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.
- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.
- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.
- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.
- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.
- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.
- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.","events_attachment":"ddx-334c0958/events.jsonl","execution-eligible":false,"id":"ddx-334c0958","issue_type":"bug","labels":["adr:024","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:review","area:workers","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","prevention","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-07-02T22:52:48Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"closed","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"closed","notes":"decomposed into ddx-044cd257, ddx-3a6f1ad6, ddx-d4817c9b, ddx-01aa7b18, ddx-1865eadc","parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"queue-rank":1850,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead worker: gate success on review evidence and classify Niflheim retry failures","updated_at":"2026-06-17T20:08:03.826920996Z"}

PROPOSED FIX

NON-SCOPE

Do not add long incident narrative or depend on the external Fizeau log path. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF

When readiness honors a prior operator promotion and skips a downgrade, the intake.blocked entry it writes is indistinguishable from any other warning. Operators cannot tell that readiness deliberately deferred to a prior acceptance.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2784-2815 constructs the intake.blocked entry without an operator_override boolean; the sibling-guard branch that detected operator promotion does not propagate that signal into the appended entry.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the intake.blocked entry schema with an operator_override boolean. Plumb the sibling-guard's operator-promotion determination into the entry construction site so operator_override=true is written when readiness skipped a downgrade because the bead was operator-promoted, and false otherwise. Consume the operator-promotion detection function provided by the sibling implementation bead.

NON-SCOPE
- rule_fingerprint field and dedup (sibling child bead).
- `ddx bead show` rendering (sibling child bead).

- Updating FEAT-010/ADR-023 policy text.
- Fizeau concrete route selection, model ranking, provider fallback, or profile catalog behavior.
- Rewriting execute-bead branch merge policy.
- Changing post-attempt review terminal outcomes that already carry concrete evidence for operator action.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. After N (default DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold) consecutive pre-claim warn outcomes sharing the same fingerprint across distinct bead IDs, emit a loop.operator_attention event and a durable operator_attention bead event (reason "preclaim_warn_repeated", carrying fingerprint, count, distinct_bead_ids, example_detail, example_payload), stop before claiming the threshold bead, and leave the remaining ready queue untouched. The threshold is configurable via ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold; zero falls back to the default.

NON-SCOPE
- Do NOT implement the CI schema drift gate (owned by the sibling child bead).
- Do NOT change verdict decoding or readiness payload schema.
- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.
